title: Who is the most tragic character in Eva
display_title: Who is the most tragic character in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Who is the most tragic character in Eva?             Quote Post by Jedi_Master » Sun Mar 22, 2009 11:22 pm New to the boards; my apologies if this has been posted previously as a topic. Who do you consider to be the most tragic or sympathetic character in Eva? Is it Kaji, who finds love in Misato, loses that care-free young lovers paradise, and reconnects with Misato, only to face an assassin's bullet? Is it Misato? Loses her father, find love in Kaji, loses him, rediscovers that love, only to know he dies? How about Professor Fuyutsuki? Had a thing for Yui, only to see her wind up with Gendo, and has to help Gendo with his plans to reunite with Yui. Doctors Akagi (both mother and daugther) had it for Gendo, only to realize he would never love them as the younger Akagi has to watch Gendo dote over Rei. Hikari, who pines for Togi? Is it ultimately Rei? Knows what she really is, knows Gendo will eventually cast her aside, that she seems destined to never find love or at least companionship in this world?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Mar 22, 2009 11:32 pm It's Pen^2. Stuck with the hottest woman in that particular universe, and he's the wrong species. Or, on a serious note, I'd argue that what we see of Asuka makes her the most tragic character. At an early age, she loses her mother in an insanely cruel fashion, she has memories of her father and her mother's doctor at the very least making out BEFORE her mother died, and her (admittedly pathetic) attempts at making friendships fail completely because no one really reciprocates, and in the end, right when she finally sorts through all of her issues, she dies. And then, she's alive again, and she's stuck on a beach with a guy who is trying to kill her and has completely lost it. And that's all there is left.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Mar 22, 2009 11:57 pm its gendo, all he wants to do is get back with his wife and women keep throwing themselves at him honestly tho, its Shinji, whiner or not, his life sucks

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 23, 2009 12:23 am Yui, for obvious reasons.

[]: Re: Who is the most tragic character in Eva?             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 23, 2009 12:23 am Jedi_Master wrote: New to the boards; my apologies if this has been posted previously as a topic.

[]: Re: Who is the most tragic character in Eva?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 23, 2009 12:26 am Reichu wrote: Offhand, no one feels the MOST "tragic" to me.

[]: Re: Who is the most tragic character in Eva?             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 23, 2009 12:37 am NAveryW wrote: Seriously? I don't see how anyone could possibly come close to Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Mar 23, 2009 12:38 am The Eighteenth Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 23, 2009 3:04 am I'm gonna have to go with Asuka, who literally never once gets a chance to truely shine without everything going to hell right afterwards. Shinji acts like he's the series' punching bag, but really most of his problems are just fallout from someone else having a much worse day than him.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Mar 23, 2009 5:03 am I would say Shinji. Or Misato... See did witness 2nd impact herself and was scarred life not even speaking for years.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Mon Mar 23, 2009 5:24 am What? You guys are ignoring the most tragic character. Do I even have to say...? Someone who admired human culture and who sought to understand human feelings, but was eventually killed (knowing he would have that fate)? There. You should be glad I kept it short for once. At least Shinji gets a happy ending because he has the chance to live. Other people aren't so lucky...

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Mon Mar 23, 2009 5:39 am Misato, if just for the answering machine scene. Everybody else seems to get to deserve their unhappiness like Ritsuko, or derive strength from it (at least temporally) like Asuka. I have trouble being sympathetic to Shinji, even though I empathize with him. But I'm one of those weird people who doesn't think he's emo or all that pathetic. At least until all his friends get killed off and goes nearly catatonic at the end, but that's wholly understandable. Kaworu had a beautiful manly death. Don't cheapen it by feeling sorry for him.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsachoul » Mon Mar 23, 2009 5:41 am I'm gonna have to go with Asuka. I would explain why, but the words keep floating away. Perhaps it is sleep time. I may elaborate tomorrow.

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Mon Mar 23, 2009 10:08 am And Rei isn't mentioned yet? -She died 3 times. -Almost all the things she did pre 3I was for gendo. Who was just making use of her. -In 3I she gave Shinji a choice--and died as a result. -Shinji and Asuka piloted their Evas with their moms trying to protect them; Rei almost got killed by her own Eva (and whatever's inside). If anything else, the whole way her character is presented made me pity her. She has a crappy life, stays in a lousy concrete box with her most prized possession being a pair of spectacles. And she doesnt have an ego big enough to make herself feel better.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 23, 2009 11:34 am Yes, but Rei became a stronger character as the show progressed, whereas Asuka's roll was to have progressively bigger chunks of her self-worth torn away every episode. Rei (and Kaworu for that matter) suffers by conscious choice, while Asuka's fall is due to her own flaws and ill fortune.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Mar 23, 2009 11:45 am The most tragic character in the show is the person watching the show. Do you people realize that watching a complete series like this has wasted 10-13 hours of your life that has no real connection to the world, then running to a forum to talk about it endlessly, wasting still more time. I'm guilty too, but the point still stands.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 23, 2009 11:56 am Time enjoyed is not time wasted.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Mar 23, 2009 1:10 pm I think they have had bad moments and for me it would be a tie between Shinji or Rei, or maybe Asuka. Asuka got her mind rape so badly and got her ass kick in End of Eva, and Shinji had a bad life as his mom died and his dad left him behind just never care for him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 23, 2009 2:42 pm There was a similar thread that was in discussion I think, arguing about "this character has more of a tough life than that one"; but I personally feel all of the characters have equally tragic lives. They're just presented in different ways is all. Also, I'm inclined to agree with CorporalChaos. It's ironic that part of Anno's message with Eva could be taken as "take a chance with life", yet here we are spending our time talking about a show more then 10 years old. Granted, we're all guilty of this but, just saying.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Mar 23, 2009 3:36 pm p06 wrote: And Rei isn't mentioned yet?

Who do you consider to be the most tragic or sympathetic character in Eva? Is it Kaji, who finds love in Misato, loses that care-free young lovers paradise, and reconnects with Misato, only to face an assassin's bullet? Is it Misato? Loses her father
